title: Modena, dopo Massolin arriva Manquant: la Serie B come trampolino
excerpt: Il Modena ha un metodo.
summary: Il Modena ha un metodo. E funziona. Pochi mesi dopo aver ceduto Yanis Massolin all'Inter per una cifra base di 3,5 milioni di euro (acquistato appena sei mesi prima per circa 900mila euro dai belgi del Francs Borains) il club emiliano ha già messo a segno il primo colpo dell'estate: Joris Manquant, attaccante francese classe 2005, arriva a parametro zero dallo Stade Nyonnais dopo la scadenza del contratto. Chi è Joris Manquant Nato a Lione il 20 ottobre 2005, Manquant è cresciuto nel settore giovanile dell'Olympique Lione e ha avuto esperienze formative anche al Metz. Con i suoi 190 centimetri ha la struttura fisica di una prima punta, ma le caratteristiche tecniche di un attaccante moderno, elegante e mobile. Nell'ultima stagione con lo Stade Nyonnais ha totalizzato 29 presenze in Challenge League, con 13 gol e 3 assist tra campionato e coppa. Battuta la concorrenza europea Sul giovane attaccante si erano mosse anche squadre come Braga, AZ Alkmaar e Heerenveen, ma il Modena ha anticipato tutti assicurandosi uno dei profili più promettenti dell'intera Challenge League svizzera. La scelta di approdare in Serie B, piuttosto che in Olanda o Portogallo, dice molto sull'attrattività del progetto costruito dalla società guidata dal presidente Carlo Rivetti e dal lavoro di scouting di Andrea Catellani.

title: Perché anche il Frosinone ha deciso di cedere le quote agli americani
excerpt: Ci siamo: secondo quanto raccolto da TMW, la prossima sarà la settimana decisiva per il passaggio di quote al vertice del club ciociaro.
summary: Ci siamo: secondo quanto raccolto da TMW, la prossima sarà la settimana decisiva per il passaggio di quote al vertice del club ciociaro. Dopo oltre vent'anni di gestione targata famiglia Stirpe, entrerà infatti in società Gamechanger 20, holding britannica già protagonista nel 2021 dell'acquisizione dell'Ipswich Town. Alle spalle dell'operazione c'è il supporto finanziario di ORG, società con sede in Arizona che amministra importanti fondi pensione statunitensi, tra cui quelli di polizia e vigili del fuoco. Figura centrale dell'affare è l'imprenditore americano Brett Johnson. Perché la famiglia Stirpe ha deciso di cedere una parte sostanziale delle quote del club del Frosinone agli americani? Quali sono le prospettive? Che ruolo avrà Stirpe? Chi sono gli uomini di Gamechanger 20? Ne parliamo nel Podcast di oggi di Tuttomercatoweb.com in compagnia di Marco Conterio

title: Thuram via dalla Juve? Servono 40 milioni. Sondaggio Nottingham, lo valuta il PSG
excerpt: Khephren Thuram è una delle pedine sacrificabili da una Juventus che ha necessità anche di vendere per rinforzare la rosa e assecondare le richieste dell'allenatore Luciano Spalletti.
summary: Khephren Thuram è una delle pedine sacrificabili da una Juventus che ha necessità anche di vendere per rinforzare la rosa e assecondare le richieste dell'allenatore Luciano Spalletti. Dalla prossima settimana entrerà in vigore la clausola del difensore brasiliano Gleison Bremer e chissà che qualche club di Premier League non possa pensare di pagarla per portare il giocatore in Inghilterra. Il centrale ora a disposizione di Ancelotti è uno di quei calciatori con cui la Juventus potrebbe fare plusvalenza: un altro è Andrea Cambiaso, un altro ancora Khepren Thuram che in questi giorni sta osservando davanti alla tv il cammino di suoi fratello e di tutta la nazionale francese ai Mondiali. Thuram nella Juventus di Spalletti non è stato una pedina fondamentale. Titolare certo, ma nei ragionamenti tra valutazione di mercato e importanza sul terreno di gioco lui è uno di quelli che la società bianconera può cedere. Ovviamente al giusto prezzo, in questo caso quantificabile intorno ai quaranta milioni di euro. Classe 2001, 25 anni compiuti lo scorso 26 marzo, Thuram due anni fa fu acquistato per circa 20 milioni di euro. I numeri dicono che la sua prima stagione in bianconero è stata migliore della seconda dove, comunque, ha collezionato più di quaranta presenze. Ha realizzato quattro gol. Sono dati da calciatore importante che hanno smosso il mercato europeo, anche se per ora nessuna offerta all'altezza delle aspettative della Juve e del ragazzo. Fin qui il sondaggio più concreto è s...

title: USA, Balogun: "Quasi frustrante vedere giocatori così forti. Vorrei essere come loro"
excerpt: Folarin Balogun, attaccante degli Stati Uniti, ha parlato oggi alla Gazzetta dello Sport della propria esperienza al Mondiale di casa: "La mia storia è speciale e unica, tutti sanno che avrei potuto giocare anche per l’Inghilterra, dove ho sempre vissuto,...
summary: Folarin Balogun, attaccante degli Stati Uniti, ha parlato oggi alla Gazzetta dello Sport della propria esperienza al Mondiale di casa: "La mia storia è speciale e unica, tutti sanno che avrei potuto giocare anche per l’Inghilterra, dove ho sempre vissuto, e per la Nigeria, ma sono orgoglioso di aver scelto l’America: l’entusiasmo e l’ambizione di questo popolo mi ispirano, qui ci sono infinite possibilità. Ora, però, voglio scrivere la storia di questa nazionale e segnare altri gol, perché quelli del Mondiale sono regali che ti restano per sempre. Ancora più importante è provare a vincere la terza gara su tre, poi penseremo al resto del torneo…". Definisce l'esperienza al Mondiale come fantastica, se la sta godendo in pieno e gli piace che la gente inizi a crederci davvero, come la squadra. McKennie gli mostra di continuo video di tifosi che guardano le partite nei bar o su maxischermi in varie parti del Paese e, quando segnano, tutti impazziscono Messi, Mbappé, Haaland hanno numeri inavvicinabili: "È quasi frustrante vedere giocatori così forti… Sono una vera sentenza, segnano una rete a partita, a volte anche di più. Vorrei arrivare a quel livello, essere costante come loro. Sono convinto di avere il potenziale per avvicinarmi".

title: Spezia, Angelozzi: "Rapporto viscerale con la città. Sono felice anche in Serie C"
excerpt: All'indomani della conferenza stampa con la quale si è ripresentato a La Spezia come direttore sportivo nell'anno della Serie C, Guido Angelozzi ha rilasciato, sul medesimo tema, alcune dichiarazioni in esclusiva ai microfoni di TuttoMercartoWeb.
summary: All'indomani della conferenza stampa con la quale si è ripresentato a La Spezia come direttore sportivo nell'anno della Serie C, Guido Angelozzi ha rilasciato, sul medesimo tema, alcune dichiarazioni in esclusiva ai microfoni di TuttoMercartoWeb.com, all'interno della rubrica 'A Tu per Tu': Direttore, è tornato allo Spezia. Sensazioni? "Ho un rapporto viscerale con questa città, sono felice di essere tornato. Questa è una piazza che mi stimola, la società mi ha voluto". Però uno come lei in Serie C.... "L'importante è andare dove si ha la possibilità di lavorare. Per me non fa differenza la categoria, ma poter lavorare e potersi esprimere".

title: Palestra to Chelsea: What Does It Mean for Italy? Has He Betrayed Inter? Video
excerpt: A new episode of the Football Italia Summer Show is out on YouTube with Lorenzo...
summary: A new episode of the Football Italia Summer Show is out on YouTube with Lorenzo Bettoni and Richard Hall discussing Marco Palestra’s imminent move to Chelsea. Palestra has chosen Chelsea over Inter and is now set to complete a permanent move worth more than €50m to Stamford Bridge. For weeks, Inter had been working on a deal with Atalanta and appeared to be leading the race for one of Italy’s brightest young talents. However, Chelsea’s financial power and long-term project have ultimately convinced the player to make the move to the Premier League.

title: Roma have buyers willing to spend €40m but Soule unconvinced by Saudi Arabia
excerpt: Reports in Italy claim that several Saudi Pro League sides are willing to invest €40m...
summary: Reports in Italy claim that several Saudi Pro League sides are willing to invest €40m on the signing of Matias Soule from Roma, but the Argentine is reportedly unconvinced by the idea of moving away from Europe at this stage of his career. It is no secret that Roma are looking to sell players before the end of the month in order to raise funds to comply with their financial fair play settlement agreement with UEFA. Several of the Giallorossi’s star players, including the likes of Manu Kone and Evan Ndicka, have previously been linked with a move away from the Stadio Olimpico in order to raise the required funds, but reports over the last few weeks indicate that Soule is increasingly likely to be sold if an appropriate offer comes in.

title: Atalanta teach a lesson in sales – €100m from Man United and Chelsea before July
excerpt: Atalanta continue to stake their claim as the finest selling club in Italy, as La...
summary: Atalanta continue to stake their claim as the finest selling club in Italy, as La Dea are set to bring in over €100m from sales to Chelsea and Manchester United, before the summer transfer window has even opened. The Serie A transfer window will officially open on June 29, but Atalanta have already secured a total €60m deal for Marco Palestra to join Chelsea this summer, while midfielder Ederson is all set to join Manchester United in a €45m deal – both figures including bonuses. There have even been reports over the last 24 hours that Brighton & Hove Albion are interested in a deal for 18-year-old defender Honest Ahanor, which could push the figure of €105m even higher.

title: Inter could reinvest Palestra funds on Nico Paz from Real Madrid instead
excerpt: Inter were left disappointed by Marco Palestra’s decision to join Chelsea earlier this week, but...
summary: Inter were left disappointed by Marco Palestra’s decision to join Chelsea earlier this week, but the latest reports claim that the Nerazzurri could now reinvest the funds they had set aside for the wing-back on Nico Paz from Real Madrid instead. Denzel Dumfries is set to join Real Madrid from Inter having had his release clause activated earlier this month, and the Nerazzurri had been hoping to replace him with 21-year-old Italy talent Palestra. However, the 2025-26 Cagliari loanee has since decided to join Premier League outfit Chelsea in a total €60m deal on a five or six-year contract that will see him earn twice as much as he was offered in Italy in wages.

title: Brighton express interest in signing Italy U21 talent Ahanor from Atalanta
excerpt: Reports in Italy claim that Premier League side Brighton and Hove Albion have registered an...
summary: Reports in Italy claim that Premier League side Brighton and Hove Albion have registered an interest in signing Atalanta and Azzurrini talent Honest Ahanor this summer. According to reports from Gianluca Di Marzio of Sky Sport in Italy, representatives from Brighton have been in Milan over the last few days for a series of meetings, and in that time, the topic of signing Ahanor from Atalanta has come up in conversations. No formal negotiations have taken place as things stand, and Di Marzio suggests that it will not be straightforward to haggle with Atalanta this summer. That is because La Dea are not pressed to sell players to raise funds given that they are set to sell Marco Palestra to Chelsea in a €60m package and Ederson to Manchester United for a total sum of €45m.

title: Vinicius Jr stops fun and leaves Scots down... but are they out?
excerpt: Scotland's party stops at the hands of Brazil in Miami, but might they still find themselves in the last 32?
summary: Scotland hopes on the brink following defeat against Brazil God bless the Tartan Army in all their beery magnificence. God bless them for their noise and colour and all-round cuddliness. God bless them for charming the good people of Boston and Miami and earning a full page feature in Wednesday's O Globo, Brazil's best-selling newspaper. They tried to lift their team in Miami but a forklift truck couldn't have got Scotland's challenge off the ground - not while they defended as they did early on, not while they only stirred and forced five saves from Alisson in the Brazil goal when they were already 3-0 down.

title: What do Scotland need to make World Cup knockouts?
excerpt: After their 3-0 defeat by Brazil, BBC Sport looks at what Scotland require to reach the knockout phase.
summary: Scotland hopes on the brink following defeat against Brazil Reaching the last 32 of the World Cup is out of Scotland's hands after they suffered a 3-0 defeat by Brazil in their final group-stage game. Scotland fans will now be clinging on to hopes of progressing as one of the eight best third-placed sides.

title: French footballer Kies, 21, dies from drowning
excerpt: French footballer Kenzo Kies has died at the age of 21 after drowning in the Rhone river.
summary: Kenzo Kies came through the youth set-up at Saint-Etienne French footballer Kenzo Kies has died from drowning in the Rhone river. Kies, 21, who played for Guingamp in Ligue 2, was in a critical condition when he was pulled from the water by firefighters on Monday, and later died in hospital.

title: The Gakpo conundrum facing Liverpool
excerpt: Cody Gakpo is thriving with the Netherlands but increased competition means his Liverpool future is uncertain.
summary: Cody Gakpo has scored five World Cup goals in seven games for the Netherlands In the aftermath of scoring twice for the Netherlands against Sweden, Cody Gakpo was asked about his role for his country compared with how he is deployed at Liverpool. "A good question. Obviously it's a little bit different," Gakpo responded.

title: 'Think we're going home' - Clarke & McGinn rue Scotland mistakes
excerpt: Scotland will have to wait until potentially the early hours of Sunday to find out if their World Cup campaign will continue, but midfielder John McGinn suspects that is "unlikely" and head coach Steve Clarke suspects they are "going home".
summary: Scotland hopes on the brink following defeat against Brazil Scotland will have to wait until potentially the early hours of Sunday to find out if their World Cup campaign will continue, but midfielder John McGinn believes it is "unlikely" and head coach Steve Clarke suspects they are "going home". Knowing a draw would almost certainly be enough to reach the last 32, Steve Clarke's side suffered a chastening 3-0 defeat by Brazil to finish third in Group C.

title: World Cup 2026: Scotland on brink after Brazil defeat, South Africa advance, and more – live
excerpt:
⚽ Latest news from day 15 | Knockout permutations
⚽ W
summary: Football, famously, is a team game, something that appears to have eluded a certain type of pundit (and consumer). And Jonathan Liew has some wise words to say about this tournament’s intrusive cult of the individual: double quotation mark“Cristiano Ronaldo’s record-equalling sixth World Cup got off to a disappointing start,” began the Reuters match report of Portugal’s 1-1 draw against the Democratic Republic of the Congo last week. And yes, OK: everyone knows how this game works and why everyone plays it. On one hand, perhaps the greatest sporting day in the history of the world’s 15th most populous country. On the other, 41-year-old man does not score. It’s no contest, really. Get those sweet keywords front and left. Harvest that delicious search traffic. Perhaps you even noticed how I just did exactly the same thing. And yet something does feel qualitatively different this summer: a tectonic shift driven partly by events on the pitch and partly at the behest of the industry itself. This is a World Cup swimming in star names, and never have those star names been so unapologetically, unquestioningly invoked. France do not beat Iraq; instead Kylian Mbappé throws down the gauntlet to Erling Haaland, Harry Kane and the rest. According to Google, Miroslav Klose’s goals record has been searched more at this tournament than in the year he set it. At times the group phase has felt like an inconvenient distraction from the real business of the Golden Boot race. (Can Lionel Messi li...

title: Vinícius Júnior leads Brazil’s star cast in blockbuster show against sorry Scotland | Paul MacInnes
excerpt:
Miami is still a city where the stars come out to play – and on Wednesday one shone brighter than anyone else

“Water so clear you can see to the bottom, hundred thousand dollar cars e’ybody got ‘em. Ain’t no surprise in the club to see Sly Stallone, Miami my second home.” So said Will Smith
summary: Miami is still a city where the stars come out to play – and on Wednesday one shone brighter than anyone else “Water so clear you can see to the bottom, hundred thousand dollar cars e’ybody got ‘em. Ain’t no surprise in the club to see Sly Stallone, Miami my second home.” So said Will Smith, and the actor-rapper’s observations ring true to this day. Miami is still a city where the stars come out to play. On this night however, and unfortunately from a Scottish perspective, Vinícius Júnior came to play his way through their defence. Just as thousands of Scots colonised Boston, so it appeared that the entire upper middle class of São Paulo had come to Florida for this big game. Brazilian celebrities had followed too, the most obvious being Ronaldinho who not only officially signed for a third division Italian side while in the city, but was afforded a VVIP spot just outside the players’ tunnel where he could greet the players, and manager Carlo Ancelotti, as they made their way out.

title: Club World Cup likely to expand for 2029 with more Premier League clubs involved
excerpt:
• Fifa agrees joint venture with lobby group EFC

  • Next edition poised to expand from 32 to 48 clubs

Fifa has agreed to create a joint venture with the lobby group European Football Clubs (EFC) to operate the Club World Cup that is likely to see more Premier Lea
summary: Fifa has agreed to create a joint venture with the lobby group European Football Clubs (EFC) to operate the Club World Cup that is likely to see more Premier League clubs enter the lucrative competition. Chelsea earned around £84m from winning last year’s inaugural 32-team tournament, leading other big European clubs to lobby Fifa for it to be expanded to increase their chances of qualification. EFC coming on board is likely to accelerate plans to expand the competition to 48 clubs when it next takes place in 2029.

title: Maseko strike sends South Africa into first World Cup knockouts with win over South Korea
excerpt: South ​Africa celebrated a first World Cup knockout berth with jubilant scenes on Wednesday after Thapelo Maseko’s second-half strike secured a 1-0 victory over South ⁠Korea, who still have a chance of advancing as one of the
summary: South ​Africa celebrated a first World Cup knockout berth with jubilant scenes on Wednesday after Thapelo Maseko’s second-half strike secured a 1-0 victory over South ⁠Korea, who still have a chance of advancing as one of the eight best third-placed teams. Maseko fired into the bottom corner in the 63rd minute to put ⁠South Africa – who will face co-hosts Canada in Los Angeles on 28 June – on four points behind ​Group A winners Mexico, who beat the ‌Czechia 3-0. South Korea, ‌who started with talisman Son Heung-min on the bench, have three points. South Africa had failed to ‌advance from the group stage in their first three World Cup appearances in 1998, 2002 and 2010.

title: Fidalgo caps Mexico rout as co-hosts maintain 100% record and send Czechia out
excerpt: There are ways of leaving a tournament. You can go out with a sheen of glory, having gone to-to-toe with a great opponent. You can be unlucky and go home raging at referees and the fates. You can self-immolate in a blaze of red cards or own goals or spectacular errors. Or you can slink away witho
summary: There are ways of leaving a tournament. You can go out with a sheen of glory, having gone to-to-toe with a great opponent. You can be unlucky and go home raging at referees and the fates. You can self-immolate in a blaze of red cards or own goals or spectacular errors. Or you can slink away without leaving a trace – and that was the path followed by the Czechia. Nobody in 20 years will remember they were involved in this World Cup, other perhaps that Ireland fans reflecting on what a mess the Czechs made of the place they pinched from them in the playoff. A win would probably have taken Czechia through but that never looked likely. The Czechia coach Miroslav Koubek left out two of his most experienced campaigners in Patrik Schick and Tomas Soucek, and the way was left clear for a 17-year-old to control the game. Soucek did come on, but then landed awkwardly and left the field in obvious distress. There had been calls for Gilberto Mora to start both Mexico’s first two games of the tournament, and it was easy to see why. He had impressed even before playing a part in the first two Mexico goals.

title: Pechova makes step up as Team England squad named for Commonwealth Games
excerpt: Jayda Pechova will make the step up to England's senior squad for this summer's Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, with Nat Metcalf and Fran Williams also named.
summary: Team England head coach Anna Stembridge has named a squad of 12 for this summer's Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, scheduled from July 23 to August 2; watch the Netball Super League live on Sky Sports Jayda Pechova will make the step up to England's senior squad for this summer's Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, with Nat Metcalf and Fran Williams also named. Head coach Anna Stembridge has named a squad of 12, including 2018 Commonwealth champion Metcalf, with Williams set to captain the team for the Glasgow event, scheduled from July 23 to August 2.

title: Raducanu told by former coach what she must do to win another Grand Slam
excerpt: Former coach Mark Petchey believes Emma Raducanu can win more Grand Slam titles if she chases happiness rather than ranking points and stops worrying about her critics.
summary: Former coach Mark Petchey delighted to see Emma Raducanu and Andrew Richardson reunited again ahead of Wimbledon; watch the ATP and WTA Tours live on Sky Sports Tennis and Sky Sports+, streaming service NOW and the Sky Sports app Former coach Mark Petchey believes Emma Raducanu can win more Grand Slam titles if she chases happiness rather than ranking points and stops worrying about her critics. A year ago, Petchey was in the middle of a successful short-term spell guiding the 23-year-old, but his broadcasting commitments meant it could not become permanent.

title: Humphries and Littler take centre stage at US Darts Masters in New York
excerpt: Luke Humphries will open the defence of his bet365 US Darts Masters title against former North American champion Leonard Gates, while Luke Littler faces David Cameron in his opener.
summary: Following events in Bahrain, Saudi Arabia and Copenhagen, the World Series of Darts resumes at Madison Square Garden, with Luke Humphries and Luke Littler both in action; you can watch the World Matchplay live on Sky Sports in Blackpool from July 18-26 Luke Humphries will open the defence of his bet365 US Darts Masters title against former North American champion Leonard Gates, while Luke Littler faces David Cameron in his opener. Thursday's opening round will see eight PDC superstars take on eight North American representatives for a place at Finals Day on Friday.

title: La reacción de Spence al escándalo del 'no saludo' a Partey: “Mi religión me guía, por eso no di la mano a Thomas...”
excerpt: El gesto del defensa inglés tras los himnos en Boston provocó la respuesta de Partey y las críticas de Evra y Ferdinand
summary: El gesto del defensa inglés tras los himnos en Boston provocó la respuesta de Partey y las críticas de Evra y Ferdinand

title: El difícil reto de ser One Club Man
excerpt: Aritz no ha podido lograrlo y sólo siete de los últimos 35 canteranos en formar parte de una plantilla de la Real tienen opciones, aunque en algunos casos parece complicado
summary: Aritz no ha podido lograrlo y sólo siete de los últimos 35 canteranos en formar parte de una plantilla de la Real tienen opciones, aunque en algunos casos parece complicado Mikel Oyarzabal, Igor Zubeldia, Aihen Muñoz, Ander Barrenetxea, Beñat Turrientes, Pablo Marín y Unai Marrero son únicos siete futbolistas canteranos que a día de hoy pueden completar toda su carrera en la Real Sociedad de los 35 que, desde el último ascenso en 2010, han logrado tener un dorsal del primer equipo después de su debut. El caso de Aritz Elustondo es el ejemplo de lo difícil que, por distintos motivos, es ser hoy en día un One Club Man en el club txuriurdin. Asier Illarramendi fue el primer futbolista formado en Zubieta en aparecer tras el brillante ascenso logrado en 2010 por las generaciones de los Mikel Aranburu o Mikel Labaka, los Asier Riesgo, Jon Ansotegi, Mikel González o Xabi Prieto, o la de los Markel Bergara, David Zurutuza, Imanol Agirretxe, Carlos Martínez o Dani Estrada, siendo Aramburu y Prieto los únicos que realizaron toda su carrera en la Real, por lo que siempre han sido muy, pero que muy reconocidos.

title: Turquía - Estados Unidos: horario, canal y dónde ver en TV y online el partido del Mundial 2026 en directo
excerpt: Todos los detalles del tercer partido de Turquía y Estados Unidos
summary: Todos los detalles del tercer partido de Turquía y Estados Unidos La selección de Turquía llega a este último partido de la fase de grupos ya sin ninguna opción de clasificación. Los turcos cayeron 0-1 ante Paraguay en un partido marcado por la primera tarjeta roja directa de la historia del Mundial por insultar a un rival cubriéndose la boca, sanción que recayó sobre Miguel Almirón, y aunque dominaron la posesión con un 78% del balón en la segunda mitad, no lograron encontrar el gol del empate. Con esta derrota, sumada a la del debut, el combinado dirigido por Vincenzo Montella queda matemáticamente eliminado del torneo. Por su parte, Estados Unidos, coanfitrión del Mundial 2026, afronta esta cita con la clasificación ya asegurada tras un sólido segundo triunfo. Los estadounidenses ganaron sin contar con su jugador franquicia, asegurando así su lugar en los dieciseisavos de final, con un autogol de Cameron Burgess provocado por Folarin Balogun y un cabezazo de Alex Freeman tras un rebote en un defensor australiano. El equipo de Mauricio Pochettino llega a este partido con la ambición de cerrar la fase de grupos con pleno de victorias.

title: Alineación probable de Paraguay- Australia en el Mundial: once del partido de la fase de grupos
excerpt: Ambas selecciones se juegan un puesto en la dieciseisavos
summary: Ambas selecciones se juegan un puesto en la dieciseisavos La fase de grupos del Mundial 2026 entra en su etapa más dramática. Esta noche, las selecciones de Paraguay y Australia se verán las caras en el espectacular Levi´s Stadium (California) para disputar la tercera y última jornada del Grupo D, un duelo crucial que determinará el rumbo de ambos combinados de cara a los dieciseisavos de final. El conjunto Australiano parte con ventaja pese al empate a puntos por la diferencia de goles. Perdieron contra EEUU 2-0 y ganaron por el mismo marcador a la decepcionante Turquía. Eso les lleva a una diferencia de goles de 0 que les da ventaja.

title: El Pau FC de la Ligue 2 francesa apunta a ser la primera prueba del verano para la Real
excerpt: El club txuriurdin anuncia un entrenamiento conjunto a puerta cerrada con el equipo galo para el 11 de julio, una semana sólo después del inicio de la pretemporada
summary: El club txuriurdin anuncia un entrenamiento conjunto a puerta cerrada con el equipo galo para el 11 de julio, una semana sólo después del inicio de la pretemporada El Pau FC apunta a ser el rival de la primera prueba que la Real Sociedad llevará a cabo en la pretemporada que Pellegrino Matarazzo dará comienzo en apenas 10 días, el 4 de julio, al informar el club txuriurdin que el equipo francés de la Ligue 2 de su país realizará un entrenamiento a puerta cerrada junto al conjunto del de Nueva Jersey. Será el día 11 de julio, solamente una semana después del arranque de la preparación. La Real 2026-27, aunque parece que sin novedades destacables, echará a andar el 4 de julio en Zubieta, anunciando el club luego el amistoso del día 18 contra el Racing de Santander y los posteriores en Inglaterra frente a Wolverhampton y Aston Villa, y el del día 31 en tierras francesas ante el Toulouse.

title: Il pugno duro dell'Atalanta (questa volta) ha pagato. Inter, che l'affare Palestra serva da insegnamento: il fattore tempo è l'unica chance contro le big d'Europa. Un altro azzurro in Premier, è una buona notizia
excerpt: Il muro contro muro, per la seconda volta consecutiva, lo ha vinto l'Atalanta.
summary: Il muro contro muro, per la seconda volta consecutiva, lo ha vinto l'Atalanta. Marco Palestra non andrà all'Inter, il suo futuro sarà al Chelsea. Uno scippo, quello fatto dai londinesi a Marotta e Ausilio, che ha spiazzato i campioni d'Italia che, fino a un paio di giorni fa, erano certi di poter acquistare l'esterno destro e regalarlo a Cristian Chivu. Un'altra beffa dopo quella di un anno fa, quando il protagonista era stato Ademola Lookman, poi ceduto dalla Dea all'Atletico Madrid a gennaio. Due vittoria per l'Atalanta, anche se diverse l'una dall'altra. Perché se durante il mercato invernale qualcuno aveva pensato che alla fine i bergamaschi ci avessero rimesso dal punto di vista economico, questa volta le cose sono andate in un modo completamente diverso, visto che oltre a incassare qualche milione in più immediatamente, l'Atalanta potrà attendere l'eventuale cessione dello stesso Palestra da parte del Chelsea per prendere il 10% della cifra che i londinesi andrebbero a incassare. Vittorie diverse, ma con la stessa costante. Come dicevamo, in realtà l'Atalanta aveva vinto anche l'anno scorso. Quella volta non c'entrarono i soldi ma la posizione che la società orobica tenne. Non si fece mettere i piedi in testa né dal giocatore, che puntò i piedi andando anche a Londra ad allenarsi da solo, né dall'Inter, che provò ad alzare il pressing, sfruttando anche il comportamento dello stesso nigeriano. Alla fine ci rimise circa 10 milioni, è vero, ma mandò un messaggio chiaro a t...

title: Calciomercato no stop - Indiscrezioni, trattative e retroscena del 24 giugno
excerpt: IL PARMA SALUTA ESTEVEZ MENTRE IL COMO ABBRACCIA KAIKI; A NAPOLI SI ATTENDE L'ANNUNCIO DI ALLEGRI.
summary: IL PARMA SALUTA ESTEVEZ MENTRE IL COMO ABBRACCIA KAIKI; A NAPOLI SI ATTENDE L'ANNUNCIO DI ALLEGRI. INTER, CHI IL SOSTITUTO DI DUMFRIES? TUTTI I NOMI DOPO LA "BEFFA" PALESTRA Il suo nome è stato accostato anche all'Atalanta, ma adesso il centrocampista belga Nicolas Raskin è un obiettivo concreto del Bologna targato Domenico Tedesco, che ha richiesto un mediano da schierare davanti alla difesa nel 4-3-3 che vuole proporre: quello del 25enne è stato definito il profilo ideale, ma ha ancora due anni di contratto con il Glasgow Rangers, e una valutazione di mercato abbastanza ingente, che pare aggirarsi sui dodici milioni di euro. Da definire poi il futuro del centrocampista Remo Freuler che, una volta conclusa la Coppa del Mondo, dovrà decidere il da farsi. È in scadenza di contratto con il Nottingham Forest, ma l'ipotesi di un rinnovo con gli emiliani al momento non appare troppo concreta; su di lui vari sondaggi, a parametro zero è un'idea anche della Roma, dove ritroverebbe Gian Piero Gasperi, che vuole alternative agli attuali titolari. E Max Allegri al Napoli? L'operazione è già stata imbastita da tempo, ma non è ancora arrivata la risoluzione del contratto che lega il tecnico al Milan: sarà questo lo step antecedente alla firma sul triennale con i partenopei. Intanto, ieri, ha fatto rientro in città il presidente Aurelio De Laurentiis: probabile quindi che, viste le tempistiche e gli sviluppi anche recenti, l'annuncio di Allegri slitti alla prossima settimana.

title: Palestra vola a Londra, l'Atalanta sorride. Inter infastidita, ora aspetta Nico Paz
excerpt: Se c’era qualche dubbio, l’ultima giornata l’ha spazzato via.
summary: Se c’era qualche dubbio, l’ultima giornata l’ha spazzato via. Nessun rilancio, tanto fastidio: l’Inter, giocoforza, molla Marco Palestra. Informata del sì del laterale italiano al Chelsea, la dirigenza interista è rimasta dapprima spiazzata, poi decisamente infastidita, infine ha iniziato a sfogliare i petali della margherita. Le alternative vanno da Cambiaso a Ndoye, passando per Dodo e Kayode. Chi sorride, oltre al giocatore che guadagnerà cinque milioni di euro all’anno a salire per i prossimi cinque anni, è l’Atalanta. Il club orobico incasserà 55 milioni di euro più 3 di bonus e il 10% sull’eventuale futura rivendita, una cifra record per un terzino. Soldi che Cristiano Giuntoli potrebbe versare su un clamoroso ritorno: la Dea sta infatti pensando a Franck Kessie. Palestra, invece, nelle prossime ore volerà alla corte di Xabi Alonso. Quanto all’Inter, ora non resta che attendere. Nelle prossime ore il ds del Como, Carlalberto Ludi, viaggerà a Madrid per capire i margini di fattibilità della conferma di Nico Paz. Se le discussioni con il Real naufragheranno, è pronto a inserirsi il club nerazzurro, che insegue da tempo l’argentino e potrebbe spendere per lui quel che ha malvolentieri risparmiato su Palestra.

title: Como, ecco Kaiki. Il brasiliano è arrivato: "Giocare per Fabregas è magico"
excerpt: Kaiki Bruno da Silva, più noto semplicemente come Kaiki, è pronto a iniziare la sua nuova avventura in Italia.
summary: Kaiki Bruno da Silva, più noto semplicemente come Kaiki, è pronto a iniziare la sua nuova avventura in Italia. Il laterale brasiliano classe 2003, arrivato nella giornata appena conclusa a Malpensa insieme alla famiglia, ha concluso le visite mediche e nella giornata di oggi - giovedì 25 giugno - firmerà il contratto con il Como. Il club lariano verserà al Cruzeiro circa 14 milioni di euro per metterlo a disposizione di Cesc Fabregas. Al suo arrivo in Italia, Kaiki ha rilasciato alcune brevi dichiarazioni ai cronisti presenti: "Sono felice di essere qui, di far parte di questo progetto del Como. Lavorare con Fabregas? Sì, sono felice, Fabregas è una stella per me, lo stimo molto. Lavorare con lui è un piacere immenso". Il contratto sarà di lunga durata: da capire se quadriennale o quinquennale. Cresciuto nella Toca da Raposa, Kaiki è considerato uno dei profili più interessanti prodotti dal Cruzeiro negli ultimi anni. Mancino naturale, oggi è un terzino sinistro moderno, ma il suo percorso è iniziato molto più avanti: da bambino giocava da mezzapunta e poi da centravanti. A dieci anni, dopo un torneo locale chiuso con 15 gol, attirò l’attenzione del Cruzeiro. In parallelo giocava anche a futsal all’Olimpico, sempre a Belo Horizonte, prima di essere arretrato sulla fascia sinistra nel settore giovanile.

title: Video: Alajbegovic wonder goal shows Roma and Atalanta his quality
excerpt: Kerim Alajbegovic has been linked with potential Serie A moves to Atalanta and Roma, so...
summary: Kerim Alajbegovic has been linked with potential Serie A moves to Atalanta and Roma, so he showed what he can do with a stunning goal for Bosnia and Herzegovina at the World Cup. The teenager is not set to reach his 19th birthday until September, but is already a sensation on the global football stage. He dribbled past two Qatar players and then smashed a screamer into the net from well outside the penalty area, giving Bosnia and Herzegovina the lead in this evening’s Group B game.

title: Report Hugo Bueno targeted by Fiorentina after good Wolves campaign
excerpt: Fiorentina are in negotiations with Wolverhampton Wanderers for Spanish left-back Hugo Bueno, claims transfer pundit...
summary: Fiorentina are in negotiations with Wolverhampton Wanderers for Spanish left-back Hugo Bueno, claims transfer pundit Matteo Moretto. The 23-year-old has been in the Wolves academy since 2019 and had a loan spell in the Netherlands with Feyenoord in the 2024-25 campaign. He made 38 competitive appearances for the club this season, contributing a goal and two assists.

title: Miretti talks with Bologna hit problems, Villarreal also interested
excerpt: Multiple reports suggest Juventus will sell Fabio Miretti, but Bologna and Villarreal are not close...
summary: Multiple reports suggest Juventus will sell Fabio Miretti, but Bologna and Villarreal are not close to agreeing a €14m transfer fee for the midfielder. The player turns 23 in August and came up through their youth academy, so any sale would represent practically pure profit. The Bianconeri need to make a big sale by June 30 to balance the books, especially after failing to qualify for the Champions League.

title: De Vrij ready to leave Inter and join Panathinaikos as free agent
excerpt: Inter defender Stefan de Vrij is reportedly on the verge of joining Panathinaikos as a...
summary: Inter defender Stefan de Vrij is reportedly on the verge of joining Panathinaikos as a free agent, so will not be extending his contract. The 34-year-old had been at San Siro since the summer of 2018, when he also left Lazio as a free agent with his deal running down. There had still been some small hope that he would negotiate an extension with the Nerazzurri, but it seems as if his time in Italy has come to a close.

title: Balotelli throws out first pitch at NY Mets game
excerpt: Italy legend Mario Balotelli was the star of New York City today when he threw...
summary: Italy legend Mario Balotelli was the star of New York City today when he threw out the first pitch for the Mets baseball team against the Cubs at Citi Field. The striker, who is currently playing for Al-Ittifaq in the UAE, was the guest of honour and given the task of throwing out the ceremonial first pitch in today’s game between the New York Mets and Chicago Cubs. In his career, Balotelli played for the likes of Inter, Liverpool, Manchester City, Milan, Olympique Marseille and Adana Demirspor.

title: Madibo given five-game ban for causing Kone leg break
excerpt: Qatar midfielder Assim Madibo is given a five-match ban for the challenge which resulted in a broken leg for Canada's Ismael Kone.
summary: Qatar midfielder Assim Madibo has been given a five-match ban for his challenge on Canada's Ismael Kone at the World Cup. Madibo, 29, was sent off during Qatar's 6-0 defeat last Thursday following a foul which resulted in Kone sustaining a broken leg. Fifa's disciplinary committee has now imposed a five-match suspension for serious foul play, which is subject to appeal.

title: Bosnia and Herzegovina on verge of knockout stage after seeing off Qatar
excerpt: Bosnia and Herzegovina always knew boosting their goals-scored column would enhance their chances of advancing to the knockout stage for the first time. It was no wonder, then, that thousands of their supporters went berserk when Ermin Mahmic added a potentially crucial third goal in a victory th
summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina always knew boosting their goals-scored column would enhance their chances of advancing to the knockout stage for the first time. It was no wonder, then, that thousands of their supporters went berserk when Ermin Mahmic added a potentially crucial third goal in a victory that eliminated Qatar. The goal of the game undoubtedly belonged to the exciting 18-year-old Kerim Alajbegovic, but Mahmic’s sealed the win, third in Group B and a probable last-32 date with the USA in Santa Clara, at the home of the San Francisco 49ers. There were jubilant scenes at the final whistle. Sead Kolasinac, one of only two players from their last World Cup appearance in 2014, clenched his fists as the other, Edin Dzeko, after his 150th cap, embraced staff, bear hugs the order of the day. Mahmic, a 21-year-old who until May was still an Austrian youth international, was in tears. It was the same in the stands, where one supporter held aloft his blue-and-yellow scarf, lips quivering, before a victory lap ensued. There was a squad photo and then both fans and players bounced in unison. The sound over the speakers? The Bosnian rock band Dubioza Kolektiv’s track USA, of course. Nobody seemed fussed about the permutations, though Sergej Barbarez, their head coach in a sharp black suit, was rather more reserved, his rigid demeanour resembling that of a nightclub doorman. “When I’m ecstatic, I keep calm, I don’t jump or sing, but it will come to me, later tonight or tomorrow morning,...

title: Canada lose home advantage for last 32 after Switzerland win to take top spot
excerpt:
With Canada’s home World Cup on the line, Jesse Marsch used his captain, Alphonso Davies, as a “decoy”. A shrewd Switzerland side, alas, never took the bait and so the switch is now Marsch’s lot as his team are forced to become road warriors.

A pulsating second half in the sauna of BC Place
summary: With Canada’s home World Cup on the line, Jesse Marsch used his captain, Alphonso Davies, as a “decoy”. A shrewd Switzerland side, alas, never took the bait and so the switch is now Marsch’s lot as his team are forced to become road warriors. A pulsating second half in the sauna of BC Place followed a dogfight of a first. At the end of it all, despite a gatecrashing intervention by the Canada supersub Promise David, Switzerland had won Group B and with it the rights to call Vancouver home through the first week of the knockout stages. Yet there would be a peculiar footnote when, after a 2-1 defeat that consigned his side to a last-32 trip to Los Angeles on a four-day turnaround, Marsch admitted Davies was never going to play a part in this finale. On Tuesday he had guaranteed the Bayern Munich star would make his first appearance of this World Cup. Chasing the draw which would have kept Canada here, Davies was unused. “Alphonso wasn’t ready yet so I was using him a little bit as a decoy,” said Marsch. “I wanted Switzerland to have to think about it.”
